title: API Backward Compatibility with Continuous Evolution
problem_statement:
A B2B SaaS platform provides a REST API consumed by 3,400 enterprise clients, many running mission-critical integrations written years ago with no active maintenance. The API must evolve to support new features (real-time webhooks, GraphQL, streaming responses, nested resource hierarchies) that require fundamentally different request/response shapes.

Any breaking change triggers costly client migrations: analysis shows the average enterprise client spends $67K and 3.2 developer-months to migrate between major API versions.

Maintaining multiple API versions in parallel has grown the codebase by 340% over 4 years, with 6 developers now dedicated full-time to legacy API maintenance.

The API must be backward-compatible with all existing clients while simultaneously adopting modern patterns that are structurally incompatible with the original design.

ground_truth.ifr_baseline: The API evolves without breaking any client or requiring version maintenance
